# Support

Thanks for using **HAWKEYE · Sterling Screening**. This page explains where to
get help and how to get a useful answer quickly.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This project is a **demo / reference build**. Out of the box every `/api/*`
> route returns deterministic mock data. It is **not** a production screening
> system and must not be used to make real compliance decisions. Please keep
> support requests scoped to the software itself.

## Where to ask

| I want to… | Go to |
|---|---|
| Ask a question or discuss an idea | [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/trex0092/HAWKEYE-STERLING-SCREENING/discussions) |
| Report a reproducible bug | [Open a bug report](https://github.com/trex0092/HAWKEYE-STERLING-SCREENING/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) |
| Request a feature | [Open a feature request](https://github.com/trex0092/HAWKEYE-STERLING-SCREENING/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml) |
| Report a security vulnerability | **Do not** open a public issue — see the [Security Policy](../SECURITY.md) |
| Contribute a change | Read the [Contributing Guide](../CONTRIBUTING.md) |

## Before you open an issue

1. **Search first.** Check
   [open and closed issues](https://github.com/trex0092/HAWKEYE-STERLING-SCREENING/issues?q=is%3Aissue)
   and [Discussions](https://github.com/trex0092/HAWKEYE-STERLING-SCREENING/discussions)
   — your question may already be answered.
2. **Read the docs.** The [README](../README.md) covers setup, configuration,
   and the environment variables that gate optional integrations.
3. **Confirm your environment.** This project targets **Node.js ≥ 20.9**
   (see [`.nvmrc`](../.nvmrc)). Many issues are resolved by matching the
   supported runtime.

## Writing a good bug report

A report we can act on includes:

- **What you did** — the exact steps to reproduce.
- **What you expected** vs. **what actually happened.**
- **Environment** — OS, browser, and Node version.
- **Evidence** — console output, screenshots, or a minimal reproduction.

The [bug report template](./ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml) prompts for all of
this.

## Response expectations

This is a community project maintained on a best-effort basis. There is **no
paid support and no service-level agreement**. Maintainers triage issues as
time allows; clear, reproducible reports are prioritised.
